title: The Effect of Hospital Postpartum Care Regulations on Breastfeeding and Maternal Time Allocation
notes: Stata: reg bf_ever Post_avg $individXs $stateXs $FEs [pw=rddwt], cluster(fips). individXs=WIC_ever i.childnm i.mom_ed frstbrn i.raceeth_cat married female i.mom_agegrp. CSDID: csdid2 bf_ever i.fips $individXs [w=rddwt], agg(event) long2. TWFE FIX (Round 3 final): Two-part fix. (1) MONTH FORMULA: Stata's `gen start_month = month(start_ym)` treats %tm values as %td (days since 01jan1960, NOT months). E.g., start_ym=600 (Jan 2010 as %tm) → month(600) = 8 (Aug 1961 in %td interpretation). R corrected using `as.integer(format(as.Date('1960-01-01') + as.integer(start_ym), '%m'))`. This causes January-policy states to be correctly classified as pre-period in the year of adoption under Stata logic. (2) styr_pergt64 OMITTED: feols drops a different variable than Stata when all styr_per* are included (Stata omits styr_pergt64 due to collinearity with other proportions). Explicit removal from twfe_controls gives exact match. Combined fix: R feols = 0.03832 (exact match). CSDID-NT: 0.01652 vs Stata 0.01799 (8.2% gap). Pattern 25 applied. BJS: approximation via bjs_aggregate_to_panel. EVENT STUDY (es76_script.R): TWFE ES: EXACT MATCH with Stata (all 10 periods, gaps < 0.1%). Key: m_treat=byear-start_year (no month correction), FEs=fips+byear+childnm+mom_ed+raceeth_cat+mom_agegrp (NO agegrp — only in PE not ES), styr_pergt64 omitted. CS-NT ES: large gaps (unfixable — Stata includes i.fips as covariate causing ~50 extra state FEs, R att_gt cannot include state FEs without singularity). SA ES: 5-55% gaps, VCOV near-PSD warning (cohort identification via gvar_CS differs from Stata eventstudyinteract). BJS ES: tau2(-6.8%), tau3(+5.7%) match well; tau0/tau1 differ (aggregation vs Stata individual-level).
